response.justification: The candidate brings roughly 20 years of broad software engineering experience and recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent tooling and MCP frameworks, but falls well short of the core requirements for this director-level AI strategy role. The position demands recognized AI expertise, a track record of building AI-powered products or platforms at scale, and the strategic advisory presence expected of a principal-level IC. Substantive AI work appears only in the current role (started April 2025), there is no cloud architecture leadership, and the resume consistently reflects senior individual contributor output rather than the customer-facing strategic influence, thought leadership, or organizational shaping this role requires.
strengths: 20+ years of diverse hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems demonstrates genuine technical breadth
Founding and sustaining GISCollective as a production open-source geospatial platform shows entrepreneurial initiative and end-to-end product ownership
Current work on MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks signals early, practical engagement with AI infrastructure tooling
concerns: AI expertise is nascent — substantive AI/agent work is limited to one role started in April 2025, with no demonstrated history of building or shipping AI-powered products at scale

No evidence of director-level behaviors: advising strategic customers or partners, shaping product or platform roadmap at an organizational level, or recognized external thought leadership in AI or cloud
Cloud experience is shallow — using AWS CDK on a pricing team does not constitute the cloud platform expertise required to advise enterprise partners on cutting-edge cloud and AI adoption strategies

key_factors.factor: AI expertise depth and track record
Years and breadth of engineering experience
